title: Protein Peanut Butter Cups
summary: These high-protein peanut butter cups will satisfy your sweet tooth. They're an easy, no-bake high-protein snack.

method.description: Make the peanut butter filling. Warm the peanut butter in the microwave for 20–30 secs until runny. Stir in the protein powder until smooth and thick.
In a heatproof bowl, melt the dark chocolate and coconut oil together until glossy — either in short microwave bursts or over a pan of simmering water.

Assemble the cups. Line a mini muffin tin or silicone mould with paper cases. Spoon 1 tsp of melted chocolate into the base of each case. Add 1–2 tsp of the peanut butter filling, then cover with another spoonful of melted chocolate.
Transfer to the fridge or freezer and chill for at least 1 hour, or until fully set.
To serve, peel from the cases and enjoy straight from the fridge or slightly softened at room temp.

tips: Fridge: Keeps for up to 1 week in an airtight container.
Freezer: Freeze for up to 3 months. Store in a sealed container, separating layers with baking paper.
To eat: Defrost for 10–15 mins before eating, or enjoy frozen for a firmer bite.

chefs.name: Finn Tonry
chefs.summary: Finn Tonry (@Finntonry) is a chef and recipe developer who’s all about making healthy food exciting. After years working in some of London’s best restaurants, he’s now focused on creating high-protein dishes that look great, taste amazing, and actually make you feel good.
